title: Goemans' Open Problem on Spanning Tree Extension Complexity
statement_text: Improve either the lower bound $\mathrm{xc}(P(G)) \geq |E(G)| - |V(G)|$ or the upper bound $\mathrm{xc}(P(G)) \in O(|V(G)| \cdot |E(G)|)$ for the extension complexity of the spanning tree polytope of a connected graph $G$.
context_text: Wong (1980) and Martin (1991) proved the $O(|V(G)| \cdot |E(G)|)$ upper bound for every connected graph $G$, while the only known lower bound is $|E(G)| - |V(G)|$ (the dimension of the polytope). Michel Goemans posed the open problem of improving either bound; the paper describes it as notoriously difficult and still wide open, citing Khoshkhah and Theis for further discussion.
paper_contribution: Proves $\mathrm{xc}(P(G)) \in O(n^{3/2})$ for every connected $n$-vertex graph $G$ in any proper minor-closed class, and more generally $\mathrm{xc}(P(G)) \in O(n^{1+\beta})$ for graph classes closed under induced subgraphs that admit $\frac{1}{2}$-balanced separators of size $O(n^{\beta})$, strictly improving the $O(n^2)$ bound for all such classes.
notes: PDF source — problem is stated in prose without a labelled theorem environment. Section 5 ('some open problems' as announced by the authors) was not recoverable from the PDF extraction, so any additional open problems or conjectures posed there could not be captured.
paper_title: Smaller extended formulations for spanning tree polytopes in minor-closed classes and beyond
